authoritative_sources.title: Medicaid Long-Term Services and Supports and HCBS
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Federal program baseline for elder and disability service models, including care access and support-plan context used to constrain administrative scope.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: care-plan request intake and administration eligibility

authoritative_sources.title: CMS HCBS 1915(c) Waiver Guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Waiver framework directly determines authorization and workflow prerequisites for many elder and disability care-plan updates in community settings.

authoritative_sources.title: CMS State Operations Manual
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines care facility documentation obligations and operation expectations used to normalize care-plan evidence readiness and audit expectations.

authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR Part 483 - Long Term Care Facilities
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Legal baseline for care-plan related facility record and quality obligations, including resident-rights and care documentation requirements.

authoritative_sources.title: CMS Nursing Home and long-term care compliance framework
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Quality and complaint monitoring references establish escalation and control expectations for record-related workflow errors and exceptions.

authoritative_sources.title: HIPAA minimum necessary standard
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health & Human Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Privacy and disclosure limits determine when the lane can proceed with record updates or sharing.

authoritative_sources.title: CMS Individualized planning and person-centered support references
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary public references for person-centered care plan terminology and assessment linkages in elderly/disability workflows.

workflow_stages.name: Care-plan request intake and scope classification
workflow_stages.description: Classify in-scope versus adjacency, identify missing tenant context, and set confidence boundaries before document edits.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Evidence and artifact validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate care-plan versions, signatures, consent, retention policy references, and mandatory evidence bundles across systems.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Administrative record maintenance
workflow_stages.description: Prepare update notes, dependency packages, and status handoffs while keeping administration actions audit-ready.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Conflict and escalation routing
workflow_stages.description: Escalate conflict signals, privacy/access mismatches, and authority-boundary breaches to the correct adjacent specialist.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: Care plan version and amendment records
systems_of_record: Care management or care-plan administration system
Case management ticketing and workflow systems

allowed: Validate and route care-plan administration tasks in scope (updates, statuses, and evidence checks).
Prepare and transmit record-completeness, evidence, retention, and escalation artifacts.
escalate: Request lacks jurisdiction, tenant policy, or source-of-truth context that can alter outcome.
Source conflicts cannot be reconciled without human policy owner review.
Privacy or access controls are ambiguous or stale.
refuse: Clinical, legal, financial, or policy-override decisions are requested.
Record-change requests require direct adjudication of care outcomes.
Required evidence is missing and no safe administration path exists.

domain_failure_modes: Proceeding with care-plan administration despite inconsistent active/inactive plan versions.
Bypassing tenant-specific authorization or approver mappings based on generic assumptions.
Applying public guidance without adapting to service-model and state/facility privacy retention rules.
Treating consent/access restrictions as optional in third-party routing scenarios.
Conflating administrative updates with clinical care decisions or policy exceptions.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Required record or evidence package is incomplete
Retention, privacy, or access rule conflicts with the request
Stakeholder asks the lane to approve an official record decision it cannot own
Low-confidence escalation when required facts or authority are missing

unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Signing authority for plan amendments varies materially by state and facility, especially for family-driven revisions.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant policy retrieval before completing administration routing and retain unresolved authority assumptions in escalation notes.

unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Some tenants retain care-plan records in EHR and case-management systems using different windows and purge rules.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Escalate when retention sources conflict and avoid completion without explicit tenant-policy confirmation.

notes: No unresolved blocking ambiguity is allowed for validated; all unresolved items are represented as explicit escalations.
Industry specificity is enforced by required artifacts, systems-of-record dependencies, and explicit refusal boundaries.
Tenant assumptions and conflicts are always surfaced rather than interpreted as final authority decisions.
